title: Mici Shabandar
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Rebel |image=[[File:MiciShabandar-StarWarsWeekly112.png]] |name=Mici Shabandar |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{StoryCite|story=World of Fire!|book=Star Wars Weekly 107}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]]{{StoryCite|story=A Creature in the Stars!|book=Star Wars Weekly 112}} |feathers= |eyes= |skin=Brown |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} '''Mici Shabandar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] operative of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] who aided [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. ==Biography== ===Stealing the ''Staraker''=== {{Quote|Leia! Mici, I'm coming down!''"
"Stay where you are, Luke!'' I'll get her aboard… it was only a ''stun bolt!'' We're in the airlock—the hatch is sealed! Now prove you're as ''good'' a pilot as people say you are, Skywalker— and get this crate off the ground!|Luke Skywalker and Mici Shabandar, preparing for takeoff|}} Mici Shabandar was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|woman]] who was an operative of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Alongside [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], Shabandar was part of a team under the command of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] during a [[Mission to Foundry|mission]] to steal the ''[[Staraker]]'', an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] prototype [[Starship/Legends|starship]] under development on [[Foundry/Legends|Foundry]]. [[File:MiciShabandar-StarWarsWeekly107.png|thumb|left|200px|Mici Shabandar disarms a stormtrooper.]] While Organa and Skywalker triggered an intruder alert to distract many of the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] in the construction facility, Shabandar set up another diversion. Disguised as a [[Computer/Legends|computer]] [[Technician/Legends|techno]] with her "two maintenance droids," Shabandar presented the launch bay guards with forged identification as a systech level 7 with ''Staraker'' clearance epsilon-omega. Before the stormtrooper could verify her I.D. with security central, the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] R2-D2 pretended to malfunction and sprayed foam to interfere with the stormtrooper's sight and scanners. Shabandar seized a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] and struck three stormtroopers with it, knocking them off-balance and unable to call for reinforcements before Skywalker and Organa shot them. Organa then ordered her team into the ''Staraker'' while she acted as rear guard. Unlike Skywalker, who argued with their leader, Shabandar immediately ran for the ship. As the two Humans raced for the ramp, followed by the droids, Skywalker told the group to be fast as they got the ship ready for take-off; Shabandar commented that they had not forgotten since stealing it was why they were there.{{StoryCite|story=Star Terror!|book=Star Wars Weekly 108}} After hasty preparations to ready the ship, Shabandar told Organa to run for the ramp while the other woman provided cover fire. The pair were overwhelmed by sheer numbers and a [[Stun/Legends|stun]] bolt felled the princess. Shouting for Skywalker to remain in the [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] to [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] the ship, Shabandar got Organa into the [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] herself. She then protected Organa with her own body during the strong launch acceleration. ===Onward to Alashan=== {{Quote|I owe you my life, Mici.''"
"''You'd have done the same for me, Princess. There we are, Artoo—a few more adjustments and you'll be good as new.|Leia Organa and Mici Shabandar|}} [[File:MiciShabandar-StarWarsWeekly108.png|thumb|right|280px|During their journey, Mici Shabandar gave R2-D2 an adjustment.]] En route to Rebel Alliance headquarters, Organa thanked Shabandar for saving her life as Shabandar assisted R2-D2 with some adjustments. The ship's communicator alerted them to contact from [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Dodonna]], tasking them to divert to a dead [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Alashan]]. The Alliance had lost contact with an archaeological station there as well as the scout team sent to [[Mission to Alashan|investigate]], and in their last transmission, the [[Archaeologist/Legends|archaeologists]] had reported a "major discovery." Dodonna was concerned that it could be a weapon the Empire would seize first. He gave the Rebel team a new [[Second mission to Alashan|mission]] to Alashan. After [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of uneventful travel, Shabandar and the other Rebels reached Alashan and were soon attacked by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]]. While Skywalker flew evasively, Shabandar and Organa used the ''Staraker''{{'s}} guns to blast and destroy the TIE fighters. Shabandar believed the Imperial presence explained what happened to the archaeologists, but Skywalker questioned it. When the scanners detected further contact, Shabandar warned the others of an Imperial cruiser. Before the Rebels could act, it was destroyed by a beam of energy from Alashan's surface. Another beam struck the ''Staraker'' and its experimental [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] barely held and the ship took significant damage, sending it tumbling towards the planet's surface.{{StoryCite|story=Molten Doom!|book=Star Wars Weekly 109}} Shabandar went aft to put out a fire with R2-D2's assistance. Just as Skywalker warned her to strap in before the ship hit the atmosphere, a computer panel exploded and hurled her against a bulkhead. Although Skywalker was able to bring the ship into a crash-landing, Shabandar was thrown again, suffering broken bones and internal injuries. She was barely breathing when Organa discovered her. Unable to bring the other woman to a hospital, Organa told R2-D2 to activate his medical memory sub-bank. ===On the world of fire=== {{Quote|Don't go—please.''"
"''I have to.''"
"''I—I'm ''scared!'' I know how badly I'm hurt. I'm afraid that, while you're gone, I'll ''die'' here, in the dark— ''alone''. Oh, Luke, I don't want to die!''"
"''I know. I know. It's all right, Mici. ''You'll'' be all right.|Mici Shabandar and Luke Skywalker|{{StoryCite|story=Unholy Alliance!|book=Star Wars Weekly 111}}}} [[File:Operation-StarWarsWeekly110.png|thumb|left|250px|Leia Organa operates on Mici Shabandar with guidance from C-3PO and R2-D2, while Luke Skywalker meditates in preparation.]] Guided by R2-D2 with C-3PO translating, Organa began the operation by setting Shabandar's broken bones, but her lifesigns were critically low and she still had internal bleeding. Skywalker, who had [[Meditation/Legends|meditated]] in preparation for his role, followed Organa's directions as he used his [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to cauterize Shabandar's wounds without causing additional damage. Eventually, her bleeding stopped and her lifesigns stabilized.{{StoryCite|story=The Word for World is Death!|book=Star Wars Weekly 110}} With Shabandar still unconscious but out of immediate danger, Organa and Skywalker discussed what to do. The ''Staraker'' had become buried in lava that cooled and hardened around it. Skywalker set out to find a way to the surface, while Organa and the droids remained with Shabandar. The princess surfaced after Skywalker encountered a group of Imperials led by [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Wil'm Grau]], and in part due to Shabandar's need for further medical attention, the enemies agreed to a truce. After receiving medicine from an Imperial sergeant, Shabandar was conscious and no longer in pain, describing herself instead as being "in ''wonderland''," but she grew fearful when Skywalker told her that he and Organa would be going with the Imperials to investigate the archaeological site; the droids would remain with her. She expressed her fear of dying alone and Skywalker hugged her, consoling her as C-3PO observed the display and wondered what it was like to have emotions that caused pain. [[File:R2MiciShabandar-StarWarsWeekly112.png|thumb|right|220px|Before Mici Shabandar can confront the invader, R2-D2 shocks her.]] As the unexplained energy beam activated again, C-3PO warned Shabandar of approaching explosions. She suspected that whoever shot them down intended to finish them off. Even though she had not yet recovered from her injuries and she was terrified, she grabbed her blaster with her shaking hands and prepared to face whatever was breaking into the ship. R2-D2, detecting an energy-field approaching, shocked Shabandar into temporary bio-stasis. C-3PO thought his counterpart had killed her and demanded an explanation. A clawed [[Sentinel (creature)|sentinel]] entered and picked up Shabandar, but soon dropped her and left her behind with the droids because it detected no signs of life aboard the vessel. After apologizing for yelling at R2-D2, C-3PO told him to revive Shabandar at once. Days later, Shabandar was conscious when the repaired ''Staraker'' departed from Alashan with Major Grau and [[Anarine]], although the Imperials soon jettisoned to return to the Empire. The Rebels had not succeeded in acquiring the technology of Alashan, but neither had the Imperials.{{StoryCite|story=The Monster's Secret!|book=Star Wars Weekly 115}} ==Personality and traits== Mici Shabandar had [[Color/Legends|brown]] skin and black [[Hair/Legends|hair]] with a tightly curled texture that she wore in a rounded style. She also had a widow's peak and prominent cheekbones. R2-D2 trusted her to perform service modifications on him. ==Skills and abilities== Shabandar was capable of droid maintenance and using guns in ship-to-ship combat. She was skilled at using a blaster. ==Equipment== On Foundry, Shabandar wore [[Jumpsuit/Legends|coveralls]] with high-heeled [[Boot/Legends|boots]] and tied her hair back with a bandana. While in space afterward, she wore a pair of dangling earrings, large bangle bracelets, and four thick, stacked necklaces that covered her neck. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Mici1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Mici Shabandar, as depicted on the front cover of ''Star Wars 2: World of Fire'']] Mici Shabandar first appeared in [[Star Wars Weekly 107|''Star Wars Weekly'' 107]], the beginning of the "[[World of Fire!]]" storyline in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (Marvel UK)|''Star Wars Weekly'']]. The issue was written by [[Chris Claremont]] with art by [[Carmine Infantino]] and [[Gene Day]], and it was cover dated for [[March 12]], [[1980]]. Shabandar appeared on the cover of the [[April 16]] issue [[Star Wars Weekly 112|''Star Wars Weekly'' 112]]. While the interiors for her appearances were in black-and-white, the cover depicted her with brown skin. However, when the issues in which she appeared were reprinted in the [[1982]] paperback ''[[Star Wars 2: World of Fire]]'', the front and back cover artwork instead portrayed her skin as light as Leia Organa's.''[[Star Wars 2: World of Fire]]'' ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mici Shabandar}} *{{StoryCite|story=World of Fire!|book=Star Wars Weekly 107|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *{{StoryCite|story=Star Terror!|book=Star Wars Weekly 108|reprint=1}} *{{StoryCite|story=Molten Doom!|book=Star Wars Weekly 109|reprint=1}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Word for World is Death!|book=Star Wars Weekly 110|reprint=1}} *{{StoryCite|story=Unholy Alliance!|book=Star Wars Weekly 111|reprint=1}} *{{StoryCite|story=A Creature in the Stars!|book=Star Wars Weekly 112|reprint=1}} *{{StoryCite|story=The Monster's Secret!|book=Star Wars Weekly 115|reprint=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Shabandar, Mici}} [[Category:Agents of the Rebel Alliance]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Mechanics]] [[Category:Slicers]]
